name: Palomares de Yelo by the Tierra de Medinaceli — circular by Baraona
name_original: Palomares de Yelo por la Tierra de Medinaceli — circular por Baraona
description: This moderate 53.4-mile road cycling route through Tierra de Medinaceli offers historical scenery, vast cereal fields, and ancient fortresse
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Miño De Medinaceli traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, historical towns, and rolling terrain. The region offers varied topography suitable for road cycling, with routes often connecting ancient settlements and following natural waterways like the Río Jalón. This area provides a mix of ascents and descents, allowing for diverse cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Miño De Medinaceli The most popular road cycling route is Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular, a 59.7 miles (96.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and explores the area around the historic town of Medinaceli and the Jalón River. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Palomares de Yelo by the Tierra de Medinaceli — circular by Baraona, a moderate 53.4 miles (85.9 km) path. This route explores the "Land of Medinaceli" and features historical dovecotes. Local road cyclists also love the Medinaceli – Sigüenza loop from Medinaceli, a 37.6 miles (60.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain between two historic towns, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Miño De Medinaceli is defined by river valleys, historical towns, and rolling agricultural lands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Miño De Medinaceli are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 60 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Miño De Medinaceli's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Miño De Medinaceli?
A: There are 12 road cycling routes around Miño De Medinaceli, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Yes, the region offers one easy road cycling route. While specific beginner routes are fewer, many of the moderate routes can be approached by cyclists with some experience, especially those looking for a gentle introduction to the area's rolling terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077550648/palomares-de-yelo-by-the-tierra-de-medinaceli-circular-by-baraona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomares de Yelo by the Tierra de Medinaceli — circular by Baraona&lt;/a&gt; route is a popular moderate loop.

Q: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect on road cycling routes here?
A: Road cycling routes around Miño De Medinaceli often feature historical towns, river valleys, and rolling agricultural lands. You can pass by significant landmarks such as the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, there are two difficult road cycling routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077496740/medinaceli-and-rio-jalon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, is a challenging 96.1 km ride with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Miño De Medinaceli?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied topography, which includes a mix of ascents and descents, and the opportunity to explore ancient settlements and natural waterways like the Río Jalón.

Q: Are there any routes that connect historical towns?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history, and routes often link historical towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli – Sigüenza loop from Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; takes you through varied terrain between these two significant historical centers.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in this area?
A: Route durations vary depending on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza – Sigüenza Castle loop from Alcuneza&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 46 minutes, while longer, more difficult routes can take over 4 hours.

Q: Can I find routes that feature unique local characteristics, like historical architecture?
A: Yes, the region is known for its historical elements. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yelo – Ancient Dovecotes of Yelo loop from Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the historical dovecotes, offering a glimpse into the local heritage.

Q: Are there any natural attractions to see while cycling?
A: Beyond historical sites, the area also features natural monuments. You might encounter unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pared&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6885972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Peñichuela&lt;/a&gt;, or even the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7849684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de los Corrales de Sinova&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Beautiful town that is located close to Medinaceli and that is characterized by buildings called Palomares, which you can see when you pass through the town!
Before or after reaching the town of Baraona there are long straights where in its surroundings we find cultivated fields and small hills that rise close to the road.
Between the towns of Conquezuela and Miño de Medinaceli there is the Ermita de la Virgen de la Santa Cruz where behind it is what is called La Pared, a set of rocks that rise next to it road
Road that descends from the town of Torralba del Moral and where it goes in the direction of Sigüenza, passing by the Río de Henares and the foothills of the Sierra Ministra!

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as an intermediate road ride. It covers a distance of 85.9 kilometers with an elevation gain of approximately 850 meters, requiring good fitness to complete.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Palomares de Yelo circular route?
faqs.answer: You can expect largely well-paved surfaces that are easy to ride, making it suitable for road cycling. While the broader Tierra de Medinaceli region can have varied terrain, this specific route is known for its favorable road conditions.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and scenic spots will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route immerses you in a historically rich area. You'll traverse the scenic plains of the Castilian plateau, dotted with vast cereal fields and imposing fortresses. The nearby town of Medinaceli is a significant highlight, featuring a Roman Arch, an Arab Gate, and the Collegiate Church. You'll also pass by the Yelo settlement, the Baraona Straights, La Pared, and the Sierra Ministra Road.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its intermediate difficulty and requirement for good fitness, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience and endurance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot on the Castilian plateau, and winters may bring challenging weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Palomares de Yelo circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route, you can typically find parking in the towns or villages along the route, such as Baraona or Yelo, to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available near town centers.

faqs.question: Is the Palomares de Yelo circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is on public roads, it is primarily designed for road cycling. Bringing dogs, especially off-leash, is not recommended due to traffic and the nature of the terrain. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured and does not interfere with other cyclists or local wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several small towns and villages within the Tierra de Medinaceli region, such as Yelo. You may find local cafes, restaurants, or small accommodations in these settlements, particularly in larger towns like Medinaceli, which is circled by the route.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the antiguo trazado Madrid-Barcelona, Calle de la Fuente, Camino de Medinaceli, and the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day of cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and intermediate difficulty, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your racebike, and appropriate cycling attire. Sun protection is also recommended, especially during warmer months, as much of the route is exposed.
